heading: Intoxicating liquor in Indian country as evidence of unlawful introduction
label: 3488 Intoxicating liquor in Indian country as evidence of unlawful introduction
text: The possession by a person of intoxicating liquors in Indian country where the introduction is prohibited by treaty or Federal statute shall be prima facie evidence of unlawful introduction.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on , U.S.C., 1940 ed., Indians (, ).section 245 of title 25May 18, 1916, ch. 125, § 139 Stat. 124The only change made was the insertion of the word “Indian” before “country”, to substitute specificity for generality. (See definition of “Indian country” in .)section 1151 of this title
